title: Epic: host-asterinas-workspace — bring the asterinas planner onto jjforge
body:
Goal: host the asterinas-workspace project (~968
markdown-file "issues" under `~/p/asterinas-workspace/issues/`)
on jjforge. End state: `jjf ready` against the asterinas
planner picks up the right next ticket; subagents work it;
the markdown tree becomes a read-only archive.

# Approach

Two phases, neither of which builds speculative features.

## Phase 1: schema-fit audit (no code)

Read the existing asterinas issues end-to-end. Every field,
tag, convention, and ad-hoc taxonomy they use today must
have an answer to "how would I query this in jjforge?"
The answer is either:

- **Already covered.** Maps cleanly to type / slug / status /
  labels / parent-child edges.
- **Loses information.** Map to a label as the lossy
  fallback (e.g. asterinas' `Status: workaround-in-place`
  becomes `--label status-note:workaround-in-place`).
- **Genuinely missing.** File a follow-up ticket for the
  schema addition.

The audit's output is a markdown doc enumerating every
asterinas convention with its jjforge mapping. Don't add
features speculatively. Only the gaps the actual data
forces.

We're explicitly NOT trying to match feature parity with
beads. Beads' surface (await-types, deferred, hierarchical
ids, `bd prime`, MCP, federation tiers) was a useful
inspiration, but the bar for jjforge is "express every
existing asterinas issue queriably," not "implement every
beads concept."

## Phase 2: migration via fan-out

The migration is done by a fan-out of Haiku agents — many
small, parallel imports — not a custom Python script.

Each agent gets a slice of the markdown tree (e.g. one
phase's epic, or one cross-cutting subdir), reads each
file, applies the audit's mapping rules, emits a sequence
of `jjf new` calls. Agents work in parallel against
disjoint slugs so they don't collide on the bookmark.

The Haiku model is the right tier: cheap, fast, good
enough for "read a markdown file and call `jjf new`
with the right fields." We're not asking it to design
anything — the audit doc IS the design.

# Child tickets we'll file under this epic

- `host-asterinas-audit` (feature): the schema-fit audit.
  Read every asterinas convention; produce the mapping
  doc; file follow-up tickets for any genuine schema gaps.
- `host-asterinas-migrate` (feature): the Haiku fan-out
  migration. Depends on `host-asterinas-audit`.

# Depends on

- `61e9a1c storage-snapshot-cache` (CLOSED) — the perf
  unblock. At ~968 issues, the per-issue jj-log read path
  was 22.5s; the cache makes steady-state 15ms. Without
  this, the migration would be DOA.
- `b6d066b agent-dep-types` (CLOSED) — parent-child edges
  let us express phase → epic → ticket hierarchy without
  cramming it into directory-shaped labels. Use this
  epic's children as the first real test of the new
  edge kinds.

# Blocks

- The "project agent" speculation in `585b84b` is the
  next logical step AFTER this epic ships: once asterinas
  is on jjforge, an agent loop running against `jjf ready`
  becomes the live test of the orchestration thesis.

# Status

Filed 2026-06-23. Sketched. First child to file:
`host-asterinas-audit`.
